name: Hinojosa del Valle desde Llera — circular por la Ruta del Lince Ibérico
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Llera traverse a landscape characterized by the Sierra Grande de Hornachos mountain range and the Matachel River basin. The region features varied terrain, including natural habitats associated with the Ruta del Lince Ibérico. Riders can expect routes that wind through hills and along river valleys, offering diverse riding experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Llera The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra Grande de Hornachos and Matachel River Loop, a 32.2 miles (51.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 12 minutes to complete. This route offers a comprehensive loop through the local mountain range and along the river. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Camino de la Umbría Trail, a moderate 9.8 miles (15.8 km) path. This trail provides a direct route through varied terrain, often featuring shaded sections. Local mountain bikers also love the The Cave of Juan Lobón from Hornachos — circular route through the Sierra Grande de Hornachos, a 26.5 miles (42.7 km) trail leading through the Sierra Grande de Hornachos, often completed in about 4 hours 6 minutes. Mountain biking around Llera is defined by rolling hills, river valleys, and natural habitats. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Llera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 70 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Llera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of mountain bike trails can I expect around Llera?
A: Llera offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails, characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and natural habitats within the Sierra Grande de Hornachos mountain range and the Matachel River basin. You'll find a mix of moderate paths and more challenging routes, with over 15 tours available.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Llera?
A: Yes, while most trails are moderate, there is at least one easy mountain bike trail available around Llera, making it accessible for less experienced riders or those looking for a relaxed ride.

Q: What are some of the more challenging mountain bike trails in Llera?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Llera offers several difficult routes. One such option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1275093311/hinojosa-del-valle-desde-llera-circular-por-la-ruta-del-lince-iberico" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hinojosa del Valle desde Llera — circular por la Ruta del Lince Ibérico&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 67 km with significant elevation changes.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Llera?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Llera are circular. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1275033356/sierra-grande-de-hornachos-and-matachel-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Grande de Hornachos and Matachel River Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a comprehensive circular trail through the local mountain range and along the river. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1275387970/the-cave-of-juan-lobon-from-hornachos-circular-route-through-the-sierra-grande-de-hornachos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Cave of Juan Lobón from Hornachos — circular route through the Sierra Grande de Hornachos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails in Llera?
A: As you explore the trails around Llera, you can encounter several points of interest. These include historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6046777" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Oppidum of Hornachuelos&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6046737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Hornachos&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass through natural areas such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5332668" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Extremaduran Dehesas Hinojosa del Valle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Llera?
A: The mountain bike trails in Llera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the scenic views of the Sierra Grande de Hornachos, and the comprehensive loops that allow for diverse riding experiences.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Ruta del Lince Ibérico?
A: Yes, some trails in the region traverse natural habitats associated with the Ruta del Lince Ibérico. For instance, the difficult route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1275093311/hinojosa-del-valle-desde-llera-circular-por-la-ruta-del-lince-iberico" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hinojosa del Valle desde Llera — circular por la Ruta del Lince Ibérico&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights this connection.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail available in Llera?
A: One of the longest routes is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1275093311/hinojosa-del-valle-desde-llera-circular-por-la-ruta-del-lince-iberico" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hinojosa del Valle desde Llera — circular por la Ruta del Lince Ibérico&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 67 kilometers and typically takes around 5 hours and 27 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there any shaded mountain bike trails in Llera?
A: Yes, some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1275081001/camino-de-la-umbria-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de la Umbría Trail&lt;/a&gt;, are known to feature shaded sections, which can be a welcome relief during warmer periods.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on mountain bike trails in Llera?
A: The elevation gain varies significantly across trails. Moderate routes might have around 500-700 meters of ascent, while more difficult trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24528293" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Hornachos loop from Hornachos&lt;/a&gt;, can feature over 1300 meters of climbing.

Q: How many mountain bikers have explored Llera using komoot?
A: Over 70 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain and trails around Llera, contributing to the community's insights and ratings for the routes.
tips: Llera is literally in the middle of nowhere, located in an area difficult to define near the Sierras de Hornachos. Dry and devoid of trees and vegetation, there are plenty of dusty trails to explore.
Adventure along gravel roads along old cattle tracks, you will reach the ruins of a fortified Roman settlement dating from the 2nd century, situated on a small hill in the Matachel Valley.
Hinojosa del Valle is a small rural town on the Badajoz plain. Surrounded by fields of cereals and sheep farming, the fertile grounds of this valley are home to fantastic gravel trails.

waypoints.name: Church of San Sebastián de Llera
